'''''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2''' is the second, and last, issue in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi — The Freedon Nadd Uprising'']] series of comics. It was published on [[September 1]], [[1994]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=INITIATES OF THE SITH
|text=Determined to uproot Sith magic from Onderon, Jedi Master Arca orders the sarcophagi of Queen Amanoa and dark Jedi Freedon Nadd removed to the Dxun moon.

But the followers of Freedon Nadd launch an unexpected attack from beneath the miles-deep city of Iziz. During the assault, Master Arca is felled by a blow of dark Force – allowing the Naddists to succeed in capturing the royal sarcophagi.

As he recovers, Arca learns from Queen Galia that her father King Ommin is being kept alive in a secret life-support facility. Suspicious, Arca visits the dying old man with Galia and Jedi Knight Ulic Qel-Droma at his side. There, Arca's suspicions are confirmed when the spirit-avatar of long dead Freedon Nadd appears – Ommin is his follower! The crippled old King lashes out at Arca with blistering bolts of dark side energy, subduing the Jedi Master!

Ulic Qel-Droma tries valiantly to stop King Ommin from capturing Arca when Warb Null, a dark Jedi warrior, intervenes. Ulic narrowly defeats Null in a deadly lightsaber duel, but not before Ommin escapes with Master Arca as his prisoner. Realizing that he and his fellow Jedi are outmatched by the Naddists, Ulic calls for assistance from the Galactic Republic and the coalition of Jedi Knights.
}}

==Plot summary==
===Jedi and Republic reinforcements===
Answering [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] apprentice [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma's]] call for help, [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|rocket jumpers]] descend into the planet [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] in order to quell the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]]. As the [[Second Battle of Onderon|battle]] rages, the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Tetan]] royal heirs [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] crash their ship ''[[Krath Enchanter]]'' in the streets of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] but the young Ketos are able to escape with their lives.

Meanwhile, the Jedi task force from [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], led by [[Nomi Sunrider]], approach [[Modon Kira]]'s fortress citadel, which is under siege by [[Naddist]]s, [[Sith Elite Warbot|Sith war droids]] and siege machines. While the [[Cruiser/Legends|Jedi cruisers]] take out siege engines, Nomi and her fellow Jedi [[Kith Kark]], [[Dace Diath]], [[Qrrrl Toq|Qrrl Toq]], and [[Shoaneb Culu]] charge into the battle and fight through the Naddist ranks. Though the Jedi managed to break the siege, Kith Kark is killed by a stray cannon.

With the siege engines destroyed, [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]], [[Tott Doneeta]] and [[Oss Wilum]] lead the [[Beast Riders]] out of the fortress, breaking the siege. Meanwhile, Nomi finds her [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] powers under attack by an unseen adversary that strikes her unconscious. Ulic comes to Nomi's aid and reveals that their adversary is King [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]].

===Lair of the dark side===
Meanwhile, Satal and Aleema Keto seek help translating their [[Sith spellbook/Legends|Sith book]] in the cafes of Iziz. A local leads them into the depths of the city to meet King Ommin, who grants them an audience. Believing that the Ketos will oversee the "great change" that is coming, Ommin gives Satal a [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]] with which he can read the book perfectly.

While King Ommin tasks [[Novar (Human)|Novar]] with finding a scribe to copy the Sith tome, King Ommin shows the Ketos his most "prize possession", the captive Jedi Master [[Arca Jeth]]. Arca is hung on the wall, suffering under deadly [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. Ommin's goal is to extinguish his light utterly and resurrect him as a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. However, a defiant Arc tells Ommin that his [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] are coming.

===Jedi to the rescue===
Seven Jedi Ulic, Cay, Nomi, Dace, Qrrl, Tott and Oss Wilum fight their way into King Ommin's subterranean stronghold. Cay has his arm sliced off by a Sith war droid but Cay strikes him down with his lightsaber. The Jedi soon confront King Ommin, who strikes Nomi down with dark side energy. While Satal and Aleema escape with the Sith tome, Ulic slices off the former King's armature, sending him tumbling to the ground limply.

Without his iron armature to support his softened bones, Ommin collapses like an [[Arkanian jellyfish]]. Ulic demands to know where Master Arca is but Ommin is defiant even in his dying moments. Meanwhile, Satal and Aleema discover that the [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of [[Freedon Nadd]] has killed the scribe. Regarding them as heirs to the dark side, he gives them the book, the talisman, and two [[Sith sword]]s. Nadd guides them to a passage that leads them to the surface.

The Force ghost of Nadd appears beside the dying King Ommin but refuses to aid him, thinking that it is his time to die. After killing Ommin, Nadd believes that one of the Jedi belongs to the dark side. Master Arca soon appears and congratulates Ulic. Before the Jedi can deal with the dark side spirit, Nadd taunts them that the the Jedi have lost today before vanishing.

===Restoring order===
Following the defeat of the Freedon Nadd uprising, Republic forces occupy Iziz and place the city under martial law. Offworlders are evacuated to their home planets. Satal and Aleema travel offworld with their Sith treasures among the evacuees.

Master Arca supervises the purging of dark side influences from Onderon. The remains of [[Freedon Nadd]], Queen [[Amanoa]] and now King Ommin are transported to the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd]] on the jungle moon of [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]. The Jedi, King [[Oron Kira]], and Queen Galia oversee the entombment of the dark siders. Ulic asks Master Arca what Freedon Nadd meant when he said that the Jedi had lost.

Arca explains that Nadd was referring to the ancient [[Dark Lord prophecy|Sith prophecy]] that the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]] would return and that he had had heard similar stories from the [[Tedryn Holocron|Jedi Holocron]]. He urges the Jedi to cling to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]], describing it as their ally. He urges Ulic to remember that where there is light, there can be no darkness.
